attacking_style: Attacks on final climbs but excels at tactical positioning rather than pure power—sits on stronger riders' wheels (Castroviejo example) and times final acceleration in last 500m when gradient steepest.
team_tactics: EF employs him in opportunistic breakaways with numerical advantage rather than GC protection; willing to gamble from distance (flamme rouge attacks) when team has numbers in selections.
terrain_preference: Steep gradient finishes on medium-length climbs where he can demonstrate superiority over classics riders but avoid sustained efforts against pure GC climbers. Final climb selections ideal.
vulnerability: Lacks sustained power to lead on long climbs—needs wheel to follow. Absorbed when attacking from distance (flamme rouge). Non-contender on pure GC when facing top-tier stage race climbers.
key_patterns: Wins by sitting on wheels until final meters, not leading—Giro stage victory exemplifies this
Performs best in breakaways with numerical/positioning advantage—Catalunya second, multiple references to EF numbers
Attacks on final climbs create selections but struggles leading sustained efforts alone
Form improves through stage races—Vuelta 2019 climbing strength noted as improving through race
Deployed in long breakaways when not GC contender, 25+ minutes down confirmed non-threat status
form_trajectory: positive: 2022 Dauphiné fourth/sixth overall shows elevation to unexpected GC climbing depth beyond traditional breakaway role
